references.text: Biffin, E, JG Conran & A Lowe. 2010. Podocarp evolution: a molecular phylogenetic perspective. Smithsonian Contributions to Botany 95: 1-20
Earle, CJ. 1997 onwards. The gymnosperm database
Farjon, A. 2010. A handbook of the world’s conifers. Leiden: Brill.

Kelch, DG. 2002. Phylogenetic assessment of the monotypic genera Sundacarpus and Manoao (Coniferales: Podocarpaceae) utilising evidence from 18S rDNA sequences. Australian Systematic Botany 15: 29-35.
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1978. The genus Prumnopitys (Podocarpaceae) in Malesia. Blumea 24: 189-190
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1978. The taxonomy of Philippine Coniferae and Taxaceae. Kalikasan, Philippine Journal of Biology 7 (2): 118-152.
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1985. A taxonomic revision of the genus Podocarpus. Blumea 30: 251-278
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1987. Revision of the genus Nageia (Podocarpaceae). Bumea 32: 209-211
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1988. Coniferales. Flora Malesiana series 1, 10: 337-347
Laubenfels, DJ de. 1988. Podocarpaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 10: 351-419
Laubenfels, DJ de. 2015. New sections and species of Podocarpus based on the taxonomic status of P. neriifolius (Podocarpaceae) in tropical Asia. Novon 24: 133-152.
Mill, RR. 2006. A new name for Podocarpus rotundus De Laub. (Podocarpaceae). Edinburgh Journal of Botany 63: 81-83.
Quinn, C. J. & Price, R. A. 2003. Phylogeny of the Southern Hemisphere Conifers. Proc. Fourth International Conifer Conference: pp. 129-136.
Salvaña, FRP, WS Gruezo & AS Hadsall. 2018. Recent taxonomic notes and new distribution localities of Podocarpus Pers. species in the Philippines. Sibbaldia 16: 99-120

species.citation: (Parl.) de Laub. , J. Arnold Arbor. 50 (1969) 329 ; Kalikasan 7 (1978) 128; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1988) 381; ≡ Podocarpus cumingii Parl. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 16(2.2) (1868) 521.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY.

species.citation: (Blume) de Laub. , J. Arnold Arbor. 50 (1969) 320 ; Kalikasan 7 (1978) 126; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1988) 376; Coritico et al., PJS 151 (2022) 2440; ≡ Podocarpus imbricatus Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 89. var. imbricatus. = Podocarpus javanicus (Burm.f.) Merr., PJS 19 (1921) 338; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 3; ≡ Thuja javanica Burm.f., Fl. Ind. (N. L. Burman) (1768) 202; Type: Cuming 803 (MO 2 sheets). = Dacrycarpus imbricatus var. patulus de Laub., J. Arnold Arbor. 50 (1969) 320; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1988) 379. Distribution: Borneo, China, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis, Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY. Notes: Montane cloud forests, common. (200-)1300-2500(-3000)m. Native. var. robustus de Laub., J. Arnold Arbor. 50 (1969) 323; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1988) 379. ≠ Dacrycarpus steupii auct. non de Laub.; De Laubenfels, Kalikasan 7 (1978) 127. Distribution: Borneo, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, MINDANAO: South Cotabato, MINDORO. Notes: (240-)700-3000m. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, China, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Benguet, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Misamis, Misamis Occidental, South Cotabato, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY.

species.citation: de Laub. , Kalikasan 7 (1978) 140 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1988) 418; Type: FB 10894 Curran (NY or US?, holo; K, L, NY, iso), Luzon: Benguet prov.

species.notes: De Laubenfels (1988): One collection reported at 700m is doubtful. Scattered and sometimes common in cloud forests. 2000-2100m.
